basic_info.title: Pokémon Emerald Version
basic_info.review: Pokémon Emerald Version is the definitive Gen III Pokémon experience, featuring the Battle Frontier, Rayquaza as a central plot element, and numerous improvements over Ruby and Sapphire that make it the best version to play.

alternative_names: ポケットモンスターエメラルド (Pocket Monsters Emerald)

play_today.details: The definitive original version with authentic hardware experience. Features the Battle Frontier, Rayquaza storyline, and all Gen III content. Best played on original GBA or GBA SP hardware for authenticity.

recommended_patches.name: Bugfix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes various glitches and bugs in the original game including RNG issues and battle mechanics.

recommended_patches.name: RTC Clock Fix
recommended_patches.description: Resolves internal battery RTC issues that prevent clock-based events from functioning properly after save file corruption.

optimal_settings: Enable Real Time Clock in emulator settings
Use 2x or 3x internal resolution for sharper visuals
Save state slots enabled for quick saving

play_today.details: Modern emulation provides the most accessible way to play today with enhanced features like save states, fast-forward, and screen scaling. mGBA is the recommended emulator for best compatibility.

recommended_patches.name: Bugfix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes various glitches and bugs in the original game including RNG issues and battle mechanics.

recommended_patches.name: Hoenn Dex Edition
recommended_patches.description: Allows obtaining all Hoenn region Pokémon without trading, ideal for completionists and Nuzlocke runs.

recommended_patches.name: National Dex Edition
recommended_patches.description: Further expanded version allowing all Gen 3 Pokémon without trading requirements.

optimal_settings: Enable Real Time Clock in emulator settings
Use 2x or 3x internal resolution for sharper visuals
Use mGBA core instead of other GBA cores
Enable shader for CRT effect if desired

play_today.platform: Game Boy Advance (3DS Virtual Console Injection)
play_today.details: Not officially available on 3DS Virtual Console. Requires homebrew injection for 3DS play, which may have RTC compatibility issues and is not recommended for new players.

recommended_patches.name: RTC Fix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Essential patch for 3DS injection to fix RTC clock issues that commonly occur with injected GBA games.

optimal_settings: Enable Real Time Clock in emulator settings
Use 2x or 3x internal resolution for sharper visuals

description.synopsis: The player travels through the Hoenn region, collecting Gym Badges to challenge the Elite Four while stopping Team Magma and Team Aqua from awakening the legendary Pokémon Groudon and Kyogre. Rayquaza must be awakened to restore balance when both teams succeed.
key_features: Battle Frontier post-game content with seven unique battle facilities
Rayquaza as a central plot element catchable before Elite Four
Both Team Magma and Team Aqua featured throughout the story
Wireless Adapter support for trading and battling
Animated Pokémon sprites in battles and overworld
Expanded Pokédex with all Gen III Pokémon available
description.long_description: Pokémon Emerald Version, developed by Game Freak and published by Nintendo, is the enhanced third version of Generation III's Pokémon Ruby and Sapphire. Released in 2004-2005 for the Game Boy Advance, it represents the definitive Gen III experience with numerous improvements over its predecessors. The game features an expanded storyline where both Team Magma and Team Aqua serve as antagonists, requiring the player to awaken Rayquaza to stop their plans of awakening Groudon and Kyogre. Key additions include the Battle Frontier (replacing the Battle Tower), wireless connectivity support from FireRed/LeafGreen, animated Pokémon sprites, and access to all Gen III Pokémon through expanded routes and features. The game was critically acclaimed for its improvements and remains one of the most beloved entries in the main series. It sold over 6 million copies worldwide and holds a Metacritic score of approximately 76-85 depending on the source.
legacy_and_impact: Final core series Pokémon game released on Game Boy Advance hardware
Introduced the Battle Frontier concept that influenced later games like Platinum
Last Nintendo game with specially colored cartridge (emerald green)
Established many features now standard in main series games including animated sprites
Remains highly popular among speedrunners and completionists decades after release
